description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture, specled.mix_tasks

NEW lib/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate.ex: ingest/2 = :cover.stop/start/import + two analyse passes (:coverage,:line and :coverage,:function) -> module-to-repo-relative source map -> v2 envelope; {:error, :empty_coverage} on empty; unmapped-module diagnostic count. NEW lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.ingest.ex: coveralls/CI escape hatch over any .coverdata (spec coverage for free from an existing --export-coverage run). Shared MFA-string format helper with round-trip test (pins the format CoverageClosure parses). Test via child-BEAM fixture exporting real coverdata.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 14a22d4)

Store's v2 API landed in specled_-155.1 as ADDITIVE functions: Store.build_envelope/1, Store.write_v2/2, Store.read_v2/1, Store.read_status/1 (v1 write/2 and read/1 unchanged, reserved for unmigrated callers). Target these v2 names — do NOT overload or edit v1.

Spec co-change is authorized and required when the gate demands it: co-edit only the .spec/specs sections your diff directly invalidates; .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed (mix spec.check seeds it). A green gate outranks Out of Scope — make the smallest green-making edit and note it.

## Allowed touches

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/mfa_key.ex (new; or another NEW module under lib/specled_ex/coverage/ for the shared MFA-string helper)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.ingest.ex (new)
- test/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/coverage/mfa_key_test.exs (new, if helper module created)
- test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_ingest_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md and .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md (only sections your diff invalidates + stub flips)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex (CALL write_v2/read_status, never edit)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex (T3 owns the default-mode rework)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/**
- docs/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No new external deps
- No changes to existing v1 artifact behavior or the current spec.cover.test flow
- Never :cover.reset (corrupts the wrapped --cover tally)

## Verification

- Child-BEAM fixture test: a tiny generated mix project (or in-repo fixture) runs 'mix test --cover --export-coverage <name>' and Aggregate.ingest/2 of the produced .coverdata yields a v2 envelope with nonempty files+mfas, persisted via Store.write_v2 and readable via Store.read_v2
- ingest of an empty/eventless coverdata returns {:error, :empty_coverage} and Store.read_status reports {:refused, ...}
- unmapped-module diagnostic count surfaced in envelope.degraded
- MFA-string helper round-trip test (format -> parse -> identical MFA)
- mix help spec.cover.ingest exits 0 (registered task); task over a real coverdata path writes the artifact; missing/garbage path exits non-zero with a clear message
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, targeted tests, mix spec.check all green

Summary: Added SpecLedEx.Coverage.Aggregate.ingest/2 (stop/start/import a .coverdata, two :cover
analyse passes, module-to-source mapping with graceful degradation, v2 envelope), the shared
SpecLedEx.Coverage.MfaKey format/parse helper, and the mix spec.cover.ingest CI/coveralls escape
hatch task that persists the envelope via Store.write_v2/2. Co-changed coverage_capture.spec.md and
mix_tasks.spec.md with the new requirements/scenarios/verification this diff realizes; used a
Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info trailer since this implements the epic's
already-decided design rather than introducing new cross-cutting policy.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.2/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 9
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (store.ex, formatter.ex, coverage.ex, spec.cover.test.ex all UNTOUCHED)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3 (2 new tagged_tests blocks in coverage_capture + mix_tasks, run via the tagged test files; 1 dep_runtime_bootstrap grep command stub)
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - coverage_capture.aggregate_ingest: covered by test/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate_test.exs:22. Would fail if ingest returned empty files/mfas, wrong :mode, or the Store.write_v2->read_v2 round-trip didn't match (asserts version==2, mode==:aggregate, files/mfas nonempty, lib/covered.ex entry, Covered.add/2 covered). Seams: none — runs the real mix spec.cover.ingest task in a child BEAM over imported coverdata, so the imported_modules union + two analyse passes are genuinely exercised.
  - coverage_capture.aggregate_empty_coverage: covered by aggregate_test.exs:75. Would fail if an eventless .coverdata didn't yield a nonzero exit + 'empty coverage' message, or if Store.read_status/1 didn't report {:refused, _}. Seams: none.
  - coverage_capture.aggregate_unmapped_degraded: covered by aggregate_test.exs:104 (deletes Other.beam post-export). Would fail if envelope.degraded weren't true, if the mappable Covered entry were missing, or if Other.* mfas leaked in. Seams: none.
  - coverage_capture.mfa_key_round_trip: covered by test/specled_ex/coverage/mfa_key_test.exs:50 (+9 unit tests). Would fail if parse(format(mfa)) != {:ok, mfa} for any triple. Seams: none (pure).
  - mix_tasks.cover_ingest_escape_hatch: covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_ingest_test.exs:15/28/43/60. Would fail if mix help didn't exit 0, or missing/garbage paths didn't exit non-zero with 'no such file'/'failed to import', or a real export didn't write a v2 :aggregate envelope. Seams: none.
  - mix_tasks.dep_runtime_bootstrap (statement touched — exception now names spec.cover.ingest): covered by the grep command stub (PASS) + implicitly by the child-BEAM ingest tests, which only pass because the task omits @requirements ['app.config'] (its presence would purge the parent ebin). Would fail if any lib/mix/tasks/*.ex lacked the bootstrap call. Seams: none.
- Governance checks (Spec-Drift trailer): clean. branch_guard_missing_decision_update is a real finding code (default :error in branch_check.ex); the =info coded form is valid trailer vocabulary. The trailer is legitimate here — this ticket realizes the epic's already-decided aggregate-first design (Store v2 landed in 155.1; epic addenda treat aggregate-ingest as decided at admission), the new requirements were co-authored into the specs, and the OTP guardrails (never :cover.reset, :tools start) are module-local implementation posture, not new cross-cutting policy. Not a wave-through of an undocumented policy change.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched (no new deps); no edits to v1 write/2 or read/1 or the spec.cover.test flow; no :cover.reset call anywhere (only a 'never reset' doc comment).
- Targeted verification: pass. mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean); mix test aggregate_test.exs mfa_key_test.exs spec_cover_ingest_test.exs --include integration (16 tests, 0 failures); mix test --stale (exit 0); mix help spec.cover.ingest (exit 0); dep_runtime_bootstrap grep stub (PASS).
- Corpus-scoped claims: coverage_capture + mix_tasks spec edits validated whole-file (identifiers, finding codes, config keys, CLI flags, module/function names, file paths). Aggregate.ingest/2, MfaKey.format/1 & parse/1, Mix.Tasks.Spec.Cover.Ingest.run/1 all exist and match their spec statements; surface/realized_by additions all resolve to real files/functions; --output flag, default_path, empty/garbage/missing behaviors validated by execution. 6 requirement claims + task CLI behaviors validated, ~5 by execution. Falsehoods: none.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes: The aggregate real-ingest test asserts the imported_modules-union path directly (the ingesting child BEAM only sees imported data), so the :cover production-wiring claims are exercised rather than restated. The empty-coverage sidecar behavior (writing a {:refused, :empty_files} status via an intentionally-empty envelope) is a deliberate design choice, covered and passing.

title: T2: Aggregate.ingest/2, mix spec.cover.ingest, shared MFA-string helper
